title: Hemp seed face oil blend
subtitle: A lightweight facial oil for combination and oily skin
excerpt: An anhydrous facial oil built primarily on hemp seed oil, which has a near-ideal omega-6 to omega-3 ratio and a light, dry finish. Blended with rosehip oil and stabilised with vitamin E. Suits combination and oily skin types that find heavier oils comedogenic.
sourceNotes: Hemp seed oil fatty acid profile and skin compatibility: USDA AOCS fatty acid database; Callaway and others, Phytochemistry of Cannabis sativa seed oil (2010, open access). Hemp seed oil is non-comedogenic and suitable for oily skin types. Rosehip oil linoleic acid profile: AOCS data. Vitamin E as antioxidant stabiliser in oil blends: standard cosmetic chemistry reference.

recipe.yieldDescription: 1 dropper bottle (~30 ml, approximately 60 daily applications)
recipe.batchNotes: Scale proportionally. Keep the batch size small; hemp seed oil has a shorter shelf life than jojoba or argan and oxidises faster at room temperature.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Ready to use immediately. Make in small batches (30 ml) for best freshness.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: Store in the amber dropper bottle in the fridge to slow oxidation. Use within 6 months. Hemp seed oil is high in polyunsaturated fatty acids and oxidises faster than more stable oils like jojoba. Discard if the oil develops a sharp, nutty-rancid smell.

glossaryTerms.definition: A preparation containing no water phase. Anhydrous facial oils keep without a preservative because bacteria require water to grow.

An application of a small amount to the inside of the forearm, left for 24 to 48 hours before using on the face. Hemp seed oil rarely causes reactions but the patch test is standard practice for any new cosmetic product.

content.text: Hemp seed oil has an unusual fatty acid composition: linoleic (omega-6) and linolenic (omega-3) acids in a ratio of roughly 3:1, which mirrors the ratio in healthy human skin. Oils rich in linoleic acid are particularly useful for combination and oily skin, where a deficiency of linoleic acid in sebum is associated with blocked pores and congestion. Hemp seed oil feels noticeably light and dry rather than rich or occlusive, which makes it one of the better choices for people who find heavier oils like argan or sweet almond too heavy on the face. Rosehip oil adds complementary skin-barrier fatty acids. Vitamin E oil acts as an antioxidant stabiliser, slowing the oxidation of the polyunsaturated fatty acids in both hemp and rosehip. This is an
formula with a 6-month shelf life if stored in the fridge. Make in 30 ml batches to maintain freshness.

Measure each ingredient directly into the amber dropper bottle. Fit the dropper top and roll the bottle gently between the palms to combine. Label with the date made. Refrigerate immediately after making.
on the inside of the forearm before applying to the face: leave for 24 to 48 hours and check for any redness or irritation.

Apply 2 to 3 drops to clean, slightly damp skin. Press into the face and neck rather than rubbing. Use in the morning, or in the evening before a heavier moisturiser if needed. The green tint from the hemp oil is slight and disappears on absorption.
Keep in the fridge. Hemp seed oil oxidises faster than most cosmetic oils; a sharp, nutty-rancid smell means the oil has oxidised and should be discarded. Use within 6 months of making.
